meta.note: Read this file before profiling source CSVs. Re-run only if source data changes. Manual annotations added 2026-03-18: corrected test_thresholds row counts to match implemented schema.yml; added implementation_note to mostly-based thresholds (metaplane fork constraint); corrected DEF-004/005 brd_ref; updated DEF-007 resolution status; added nulls.note re tap-csv empty-string encoding.

nulls.note: Counts are from raw CSV files (generated by scripts/profile_source_data.py reading raw_data/). After tap-csv ingestion into BigQuery, empty CSV cells are stored as empty strings ('') — NOT SQL NULLs. dbt-expectations null-based tests will not catch blank strings. Use length-based tests (expect_column_value_lengths_to_be_between) instead of not_null tests for fill-rate checks on string columns. See changelog 2026-03-15 and 2026-03-18.

known_defects.description: 789 duplicate review_id values; 547 orders have multiple reviews (max 3)
known_defects.staging_fix: stg_reviews: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY review_id ORDER BY review_answer_timestamp DESC), keep rn=1

known_defects.description: Coordinate outliers: 29 lat rows outside [-35, 5]; 37 lng rows outside [-75, -34]
known_defects.staging_fix: stg_geolocation: WHERE lat BETWEEN -35 AND 5 AND lng BETWEEN -75 AND -34 before AVG()

known_defects.description: 2 categories have no English translation: ['pc_gamer', 'portateis_cozinha_e_preparadores_de_alimentos']. 610 products have null category_name.
6 zero-value voucher payments (sequential 3, 4, 13, 14) — legitimate secondary payments
known_defects.staging_fix: No fix needed. payment_value >= 0 test (not strictly > 0).

known_defects.description: File contains UTF-8 BOM. First column may read as '\ufeffproduct_category_name' depending on tap encoding.
known_defects.staging_fix: RESOLVED: tap-csv (MeltanoLabs) strips the UTF-8 BOM transparently via Python csv module. No action required in staging or pre-processing. See changelog 2026-03-14.

known_defects.description: 775 orders have no order_items rows (603 unavailable, 164 canceled, 5 created, 2 invoiced, 1 shipped). 756 of these have review records.
known_defects.staging_fix: Expected behaviour. fct_sales is item-granularity; itemless orders absent from fct_sales by design. fct_reviews.order_id links to stg_orders, not fct_sales.

stg_reviews.review_comment_title.rationale: 88.3% blank in source CSV (11.7% non-blank). Threshold 0.08 catches genuine degradation with margin.
stg_reviews.review_comment_title.implementation_note: mostly parameter NOT available in metaplane/dbt-expectations v0.6.0. Test implemented as expect_column_value_lengths_to_be_between(min_value=1) — catches empty strings (which tap-csv produces for blank CSV cells). Proportion guard is omitted; fill rate documented here as calibration evidence. See changelog 2026-03-15 and 2026-03-18.

stg_reviews.review_comment_message.rationale: 58.7% blank in source CSV (41.3% non-blank). Threshold 0.40 matches actual fill rate.
stg_reviews.review_comment_message.implementation_note: mostly parameter NOT available in metaplane/dbt-expectations v0.6.0. Same workaround as review_comment_title. See changelog 2026-03-15 and 2026-03-18.

dim_customers.geolocation_lat_lng.rationale: 99.7% row-level geo match. Threshold 0.97 gives meaningful signal on join quality degradation.
dim_customers.geolocation_lat_lng.implementation_note: mostly parameter NOT available in metaplane/dbt-expectations v0.6.0. Proportion guard is omitted; match rate documented here as calibration evidence. See changelog 2026-03-18.

dim_sellers.geolocation_lat_lng.rationale: 99.8% row-level geo match. Same rationale as dim_customers.
dim_sellers.geolocation_lat_lng.implementation_note: mostly parameter NOT available in metaplane/dbt-expectations v0.6.0. Proportion guard is omitted. See changelog 2026-03-18.

fct_sales.row_count.rationale: 112,650 source rows confirmed from live BigQuery after full pipeline run. Tightened from original profile bounds (90k–200k) to reflect confirmed data. Min 110k detects partial load or dropped items; max 120k detects duplicate ingestion.

original_profile_bounds.note: Original wide bounds before live data confirmed. Superseded 2026-03-18.

monthly_distribution.note: Computed from olist_orders_dataset.csv order_purchase_timestamp. Query run 2026-03-10.
observations: 2016-11 has zero orders — complete gap at platform launch; exclude from all trend analyses
2016-09 (4 orders), 2016-12 (1 order): platform launch sparsity; not representative
Meaningful data begins 2017-01 (800 orders, start of consistent ramp-up)
Peak: 2017-11 (7,544 orders) — Brazilian Black Friday effect
2018-09 (16 orders) and 2018-10 (4 orders): >99% drop from plateau — data cut artefact, not real activity
MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) = 2018-10-17 is misleading as RFM reference date due to tail artefact

recommended_observation_window.rationale: 20 months of complete, representative data. Excludes launch sparsity (2016) and data cut artefact (2018-09/10). Use for all trend analyses and seasonality work.

rfm_reference.rationale: Last day of August 2018 — the last complete month. August has 6,512 orders, consistent with the 6,100-7,300 stable plateau. September 2018 drops to 16 orders (data cut artefact). Using MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) = 2018-10-17 would inflate Recency scores for 20 artefact records and misrepresent customer behaviour.
rfm_reference.do_not_use: CURRENT_DATE or MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) — both produce incorrect Recency scores for historical analysis

scoring.recency: Quintile-based (1-5). Days since last order_purchase_timestamp relative to 2018-08-31. Lower days = higher score (5 = most recent).
scoring.frequency: 3-tier only. F1 = 1 order, F2 = 2 orders, F3 = 3+ orders. Standard quintile not used — 93,099 of 96,096 customers (96.9%) have exactly 1 order; quintile bins would be degenerate.
scoring.monetary: Quintile-based (1-5). SUM(total_sale_amount) per customer_unique_id. Higher spend = higher score.

repeat_purchase_rate.note: Surface as standalone metric 'marketplace stickiness rate'. Expected finding: Olist has strong acquisition but low channel retention — customers return to sellers through competing marketplaces.
